title: The One Where Ross Meets Elizabeth's Dad

jokes.text: Well, thank you in advance.
jokes.setup: Horoscope says someone special will give him a gift
jokes.punchline: Chandler thanks the horoscope itself for the future gift
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven humor with Chandler's typical sarcastic literalism. Clean structure and perfect economy.

jokes.text: You're going to make a joke about my special present. Why would you do that?
jokes.setup: Reading horoscope about lover's spat on the 12th
jokes.punchline: Chandler pre-emptively starts the predicted fight about his own joke-making tendency
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor where the character becomes self-aware of his own patterns. Strong character integration as this is quintessentially Chandler.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! It's Joey Tribbiani of Mac and C.H.E.E.S.E.
jokes.setup: Joey enters wearing costume from his new TV show
jokes.punchline: Monica treats him like a celebrity for his cheesy robot cop show
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for Joey's storyline. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I can't even use Chandler's computer, except to find porn.
jokes.setup: Joey worries about not knowing technology for his robot cop role
jokes.punchline: His only computer skill is finding porn
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey-style crude humor that fits his character perfectly. Not original but well-executed.

jokes.text: And that's only because it's there when you turn it on.
jokes.setup: Joey admits he can only find porn on computer
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's not even a skill - it's the homepage
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that makes Joey even more pathetic/endearing. Great economy and character work.

jokes.text: Our lover's spat will start a little early this month.
jokes.setup: Joey's porn comment creates awkwardness
jokes.punchline: Chandler references the horoscope prediction from earlier
jokes.explanation: Well-constructed callback that brings the cold open full circle. Good timing and structure.

jokes.text: Because the last one was such a big seller?
jokes.setup: Monica says she's writing another book
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically implies her first book failed
jokes.explanation: Standard Chandler sarcasm. Competent but predictable.

jokes.text: And as I'm the only one who has read them... I can tell you that they have all been very well received.
jokes.setup: Monica defends her 14 unpublished books
jokes.punchline: She's the only reader but claims they were well-received
jokes.explanation: Good self-delusion humor that fits Monica's competitive nature. Nice logical contradiction.

jokes.text: Wait a minute. Hold the phone. You're not Elizabeth's dad?
jokes.setup: Ross explains Elizabeth's dad wants to meet him
jokes.punchline: Chandler pretends to be shocked Ross isn't the father of his own girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke with Chandler's signature mock confusion. Well-structured.

jokes.text: We know how tough those parent-teacher conferences can be.
jokes.setup: Ross asks friends to help him impress Elizabeth's father
jokes.punchline: Monica frames it as a parent-teacher conference, highlighting the inappropriate dynamic
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation that cuts to the heart of Ross's inappropriate relationship. Good cringe humor.

jokes.text: Wow! He's so lifelike.
jokes.setup: Joey is introduced to the robot
jokes.punchline: He mistakes the human operator for the robot
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey misunderstanding that works because it's perfectly in character. Great visual setup.

jokes.text: This is the temporary robot, right?
jokes.setup: Wayne shows Joey the actual robot C.H.E.E.S.E.
jokes.punchline: Joey assumes the clearly low-budget robot must be temporary
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment as Joey insults Wayne's work. Sets up their antagonistic relationship.

jokes.text: Well, I just thought it was gonna be a really cool robot. Like the Terminator, or when I first saw you.
jokes.setup: Joey explains his disappointment with the robot
jokes.punchline: Compares Wayne favorably to the Terminator, inadvertently insulting him more
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey moment where his attempt at a compliment becomes a deeper insult. Perfect character work.

jokes.text: I prefer that Liz's boyfriends address me as Mr. Stevens.
jokes.setup: Ross calls him Paul
jokes.punchline: Immediately establishes dominance and hints at multiple boyfriends
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment moment that shows Paul's controlling nature and creates immediate tension.

jokes.text: Why can't you get a girlfriend your own age?
jokes.setup: Paul asks what Ross's problem is
jokes.punchline: Immediately cuts to the heart of the age gap issue
jokes.explanation: Brutal directness that creates immediate discomfort. Perfect economy and gets to the point.

jokes.text: It's funny... It's not funny.
jokes.setup: Ross starts to deflect with humor
jokes.punchline: Paul immediately shuts down any attempt at levity
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that establishes Paul as humorless and intimidating. Great economy.

jokes.text: Daddy. Fine. Two minutes. Go.
jokes.setup: Paul gives Ross one minute to change his mind
jokes.punchline: Elizabeth's mild protest earns Ross one extra minute
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Paul's arbitrary power and minimal concession. Builds tension effectively.

jokes.text: Not 'it.' We haven't done 'it.' I mean, we've done stuff...
jokes.setup: Ross tries to explain his relationship with Elizabeth
jokes.punchline: Accidentally overshares about their sexual activity to her father
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross awkwardness where he makes things worse by trying to explain. Good cringe factor.

jokes.text: Two guys go into a bar. One of them is Irish. I'm Irish. And the Irish guy wins the joke.
jokes.setup: Ross decides to tell a joke to lighten the mood
jokes.punchline: Completely botches the joke when he learns Paul is Irish
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe moment where Ross's panic creates absurdist non-humor. 'The Irish guy wins the joke' is memorably ridiculous.

jokes.text: ...rugged handsomeness.
jokes.setup: Phoebe says she can see where Elizabeth gets her...
jokes.punchline: Calls her father ruggedly handsome instead of beautiful
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that works within Phoebe's quirky character. Well-structured compliment subversion.

jokes.text: Is there a Mrs. Stevens? There's a Mr. Bing!
jokes.setup: Phoebe flirts by asking about Paul's marital status
jokes.punchline: Chandler immediately reminds her he exists
jokes.explanation: Perfect rapid-fire response from Chandler asserting his relationship. Great timing and economy.

jokes.text: ...because usually he's at the children's hospital. But not looking for dates.
jokes.setup: Phoebe tries to make Ross sound good by mentioning children's hospital
jokes.punchline: Accidentally implies he might be there to pick up children
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that accidentally makes Ross sound like a predator while trying to help. Perfectly botched good intention.

jokes.text: Your roommate in college died, didn't he?
jokes.setup: Chandler mentions being Ross's college roommate
jokes.punchline: Paul darkly jokes that Ross probably killed his roommate
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor showing Paul's willingness to paint Ross as dangerous. Sharp delivery.

jokes.text: A part of him did, yes.
jokes.setup: Paul suggests Ross killed his roommate
jokes.punchline: Chandler deadpans that only part of him died
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan response that plays into the murder theme while being absurdly philosophical. Perfect Chandler delivery.

jokes.text: You said we would get the kind with the little girl. You said the baby creeps you out. The little girl does.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler argue about what kind of something they agreed to get
jokes.punchline: They're both creeped out by children but can't remember which specific child
jokes.explanation: Decent relationship humor about miscommunication. Setup for Monica's book observation storyline.

jokes.text: And how people will believe anything you tell them as long as it's a compliment.
jokes.setup: Monica explains her book about relationships
jokes.punchline: Reveals her own manipulative strategy while being recorded
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor where Monica accidentally reveals her manipulation tactics. Sets up later payoffs.

jokes.text: Maybe you could date him then. It would save me the trouble of killing him.
jokes.setup: Rachel defends Ross to Paul
jokes.punchline: Paul suggests Rachel date Ross so he won't have to murder him
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Paul's hostility with casual murder threat. Darkly funny and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You just don't look old enough to have a 20-year-old daughter.
jokes.setup: Rachel tries to compliment Paul
jokes.punchline: Accidentally highlights that he was very young when he had Elizabeth
jokes.explanation: Decent awkward compliment that backfires. Sets up age-related discussion.

jokes.text: We? Yeah, it usually takes two people to... Oh, no! Yes! Of course. I know that!
jokes.setup: Paul mentions 'we were very young when we had her'
jokes.punchline: Rachel awkwardly realizes she questioned basic biology
jokes.explanation: Standard Rachel airhead moment. Functional cringe but nothing special.

jokes.text: Damn those robots. They're supposed to be our faithful servants!
jokes.setup: Joey complains about getting attitude from the robot
jokes.punchline: Chandler responds as if robots are actual oppressed servants
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist response that treats Joey's mundane problem like a robot uprising. Great quotable line.

jokes.text: There's a limit to how many sandwiches I can eat off the floor.
jokes.setup: Joey explains the robot operator's sabotage
jokes.punchline: Implies he's been eating floor sandwiches but has reached his limit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey line showing his food priorities and casual acceptance of degrading situations. Excellent character work.

jokes.text: You did. You came in, got all awkward and ran to the bedroom. You were shouting, 'Joey!'
jokes.setup: Ross pretends he didn't see Paul kissing Rachel
jokes.punchline: Paul meticulously describes Ross's obvious panic reaction
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment where denial is completely deflated by accurate observation. Builds tension well.

jokes.text: You know, he lost his keys. So he was looking for them. In your mouth?
jokes.setup: Ross tries to explain why Paul was there
jokes.punchline: Paul cuts through the lie with perfect timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and economy. 'In your mouth?' is brilliantly delivered deadpan comeback that destroys Ross's excuse.

jokes.text: You dated my sister! That was different!
jokes.setup: Rachel questions why it's weird she kissed Paul
jokes.punchline: Ross claims his dating Monica was somehow different
jokes.explanation: Weak hypocrisy joke that doesn't land well because the comparison doesn't quite work. Forced.

jokes.text: Go back there and kiss some serious robot ass!
jokes.setup: Phoebe advises Joey to win over the robot operator
jokes.punchline: Uses literal robot context with 'ass-kissing' expression
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that works with the robot context. Nothing groundbreaking but functional.

jokes.text: Yeah. That's why I'm sleeping with him on the side.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks if Joey is more charming than him
jokes.punchline: Monica deadpans that she's having an affair with Joey
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response that catches Chandler off guard. Well-timed and delivered.

jokes.text: I'm not writing about you. I'm writing about other people. Who? Marsha and Chester.
jokes.setup: Chandler complains Monica is writing about them
jokes.punchline: Monica's 'different' characters have transparent fake names
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the book storyline. Monica's obvious denial works for her character.

jokes.text: Fine. Then you know what? I will just write about Phyllis.
jokes.setup: Monica uses fake names for them in her book
jokes.punchline: Chandler retaliates by giving Phoebe a fake name too
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that turns Monica's tactic against her. Sets up the meta-writing gag.

jokes.text: I have tasted my own medicine and it is bitter!
jokes.setup: Chandler starts narrating Monica's actions
jokes.punchline: Monica dramatically admits defeat in overly theatrical terms
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic escalation that fits Monica's competitive nature. Well-constructed and quotable.

jokes.text: I can't believe this! God!
jokes.setup: Joey tries to touch the robot to show affection
jokes.punchline: Gets shocked or hurt by the robot
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy. Predictable and not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I definitely did not see that one backfiring.
jokes.setup: Ross's prom story makes him sound worse to Paul
jokes.punchline: Monica sarcastically notes the obvious backfire
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic observation. Competent but not memorable.

jokes.text: ...and partly because they just keep coming to me.
jokes.setup: Paul explains why he keeps making jabs at Ross
jokes.punchline: Admits the jokes are just too easy to resist
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Paul's quick wit and honest admission. Well-delivered.

jokes.text: 'Not all bad.'
jokes.setup: Paul gives Ross the faintest possible praise
jokes.punchline: Ross repeats it with obvious disappointment
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Ross's disappointment with damning with faint praise.

jokes.text: I know what you mean. I do that, too.
jokes.setup: Paul says he still sees Elizabeth as a 12-year-old
jokes.punchline: Ross agrees, accidentally admitting he also sees her as a child
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe moment where Ross accidentally admits the inappropriate nature of his attraction. Perfect mistake.

jokes.text: That was just a big drunken mistake.
jokes.setup: Ross tries to explain his marriage to Carol
jokes.punchline: Dismisses his marriage and Ben's conception as a drunken mistake
jokes.explanation: Good cringe showing Ross making his situation worse by insulting his own marriage and child.

jokes.text: The only way that I said 6:00... would be if I said the sentence, 'Let's meet at 7:00, not at 6:00.'
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler argue about what time they said
jokes.punchline: Monica's overly specific logic about the only way she could have said 6:00
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's need to be right with elaborate logical constructions.

jokes.text: If I had said 7:00, I would have said: 'My boyfriend is such a wiseass. 7:00!'
jokes.setup: Monica continues her elaborate argument about times
jokes.punchline: Escalates to insulting Chandler hypothetically
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Monica's argument that becomes increasingly absurd and hostile.

jokes.text: Thank you for writing your book. It's a great book... and you are the queen of everything.
jokes.setup: Phoebe demands thanks for her book before revealing the answer
jokes.punchline: Monica gives increasingly over-the-top compliments
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation showing Monica's desperation. 'Queen of everything' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: 'Marsha and Chester are mad at Phyllis.'
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler get angry at Phoebe for letting them miss the movie
jokes.punchline: Phoebe immediately documents their anger in the book
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta payoff that brings the book storyline full circle. Perfect timing and character consistency.

jokes.text: I saw you on stage talking to that beautiful woman. You know, Sarah. I wish I could talk to her. Are you in love with her? Yeah. Her. All of them. Anyone.
jokes.setup: Wayne asks Joey to teach him to talk to women
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's desperately attracted to literally every woman
jokes.explanation: Good character reveal showing Wayne's complete desperation. Nice escalation from specific to universal.

jokes.text: How to make the next one even shorter?
jokes.setup: Phoebe suggests Ross learned from each marriage
jokes.punchline: Monica suggests he only learned to make them shorter
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about Ross's pattern of failed marriages. Good economy and delivery.

jokes.text: So Ross, did you kill any of these wives?
jokes.setup: Awkward silence at dinner
jokes.punchline: Paul breaks silence by asking if Ross murdered his ex-wives
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor that perfectly breaks uncomfortable silence. Great callback to earlier murder theme and perfectly timed.

jokes.text: That's okay. I'm not so crazy about myself right now either.
jokes.setup: Paul admits he doesn't like Ross more after the outburst
jokes.punchline: Ross admits he doesn't like himself either
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating moment that shows Ross's self-awareness. Relatably pathetic.

jokes.text: Kids!
jokes.setup: Paul and Ross agree they both don't like Ross
jokes.punchline: Paul treats them like arguing children
jokes.explanation: Nice button on the scene showing Paul's parental instincts. Good economy.

jokes.text: Wayne! Wayne!
jokes.setup: C.H.E.E.S.E. doesn't deliver his line on cue
jokes.punchline: Joey has to break character to call for the operator
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy ending. Predictable resolution to the Wayne storyline.

tmdb_overview: Paul dislikes Ross but connects with Rachel. Joey nearly gets fired from his robot show. Phoebe writes book about Monica and Chandler.
